refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combines multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these should be separated into distinct FRs focusing on single aspects of worksite analysis.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide detailed analysis data specifically for the terrain of the worksite, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital representation of the worksite terrain capturing relevant features
Extract and classify key site features from raw terrain data
Create and update a structured worksite model/map accessible to planning and execution modules
Integrate safety plan and operational policies into the terrain analysis output
selected_dp.selected_dp: Create and update a structured worksite model/map accessible to planning and execution modules

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on producing a structured model/map that can be independently accessed by planning and execution functions, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of raw terrain data from sensors or external sources
Risk that integration of safety plans into the model may require additional interfaces or data formats
Assumes that the model/map format is agreed upon and stable to avoid frequent changes impacting dependent modules
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the terrain data acquisition and feature extraction DPs if sensor or data source variability is high, to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for managing and updating the structured worksite model/map, providing a centralized data representation accessible to both planning and execution submodules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data updates from vehicle sensors and sends processed model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with planning and execution modules to provide updated worksite model information and receive task-related inputs.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with external systems and modules for data synchronization and model updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the worksite model is central to the NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since it is the natural owner of the model.
Partial containment possible but would cause significant diffusion as planning modules are outside vehicle scope.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not model management; containment is weak with high diffusion.
Safety Services use the model but do not manage or update it; containment is poor with high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it centrally manages the structured worksite model/map, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion across planning and execution modules.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution if the worksite model grows large, requiring careful internal modularization.
Risk of interface overload if too many external modules depend on frequent updates, possibly impacting performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying the creation and updating of a structured worksite model/map that supports analysis of the worksite terrain. This mapping is clear and logically consistent with the requirement to provide terrain analysis.

diffusion_risks: None significant; the DP is contained within a single module (NGHE Solution) minimizing diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is highly cohesive with respect to the DP, owning the structured worksite model/map and providing centralized access to planning and execution modules. Containment scores and diffusion scores support this allocation as optimal.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through validation that the NGHE Solution module generates and maintains the worksite model/map accessible to planning and execution modules, meeting acceptance criteria for site features and safety plans.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership of the FR through the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces supporting data flow and update mechanisms.

recommended_revisions: Consider internal modularization within the NGHE Solution to manage complexity as the worksite model grows.
Monitor interface load and performance impact due to frequent updates to external modules, possibly introducing throttling or prioritization mechanisms.

decision.justification: Although the current FR, DP, and IM mapping passes all gates with clear containment and verification feasibility, the high safety criticality and recommendations to consider internal modularization and interface performance management indicate that further decomposition is warranted. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will help manage complexity within the NGHE Solution module and address potential performance risks proactively, ensuring robust safety and maintainability.
next_level_sub_frs: Create and update detailed terrain feature models
Integrate safety hazard identification and mapping
Support dynamic updating of worksite conditions
Provide interfaces for planning module data access
Provide interfaces for execution module data access

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be internally modularized to improve cohesion and manage complexity.
Interface load and performance issues can be mitigated by design changes at sub-FR level.
open_risks: Potential performance degradation due to frequent updates to external modules.
Complexity growth in the worksite model impacting maintainability and verification.
evidence_needed: Performance metrics of interface load under expected update frequency.
Verification results of sub-module functionality and integration within NGHE Solution.
